title: Kamakshi Amman Temple, Mangadu
tagline: Kamakshi Amman Temple, Mangadu — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Kamakshi Amman Temple, Mangadu, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Mangadu Kamakshi Amman Temple is a famous pilgrim destination Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Parvathi, located in Mangadu, a suburb of Chennai, India.
overview.description: The Mangadu Kamakshi Amman Temple is a famous pilgrim destination Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Parvathi, located in Mangadu, a suburb of Chennai, India. The term Mangadu means "Mango Forests or Mango Groves" and as the term implies this must have been a thickly wooded Mango groove in the past.

topPlaces.name: Velleswarar Temple, Mangadu
topPlaces.description: Velleeswarar Temple is a Shiva temple located in Mangadu, a suburb of Chennai, India. This is a mid-size temple spread over 2 acres and is among the 3 principal temples in Mangadu – the other 2 being the Sri Kamakshi Amman Temple and Sri Vaikunda Perumal Temple.

topPlaces.name: Mangadu
topPlaces.description: Mangadu is a town to the west of Chennai, India. Literally meaning a mango forest.

topPlaces.name: Sikkarayapuram
topPlaces.description: Sikkarayapuram, also known as Chikkarayapuram, is part of the Mangadu municipality and located in west of Chennai, India Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Kumananchavadi
topPlaces.description: Kumananchavadi is a suburb of the city of Chennai in Tamil Nadu, India. It is an upcoming residential suburb located between Iyyapanthangal and Poonamallee on the Mount-Poonamallee Road.

topPlaces.name: Meenakshi Academy of Higher Education and Research
topPlaces.description: The Meenakshi Academy of Higher Education and Research (MAHER), commonly referred to as Meenakshi University, is a private deemed-to-be-university for mainly medical science in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India.

image.alt: Meenakshi Academy of Higher Education and Research

topPlaces.name: Kattupakkam
topPlaces.description: Kattupakkam is a suburb of Chennai, in Tiruvallur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located in the western side of the city.

topPlaces.name: Kovur, Chennai
topPlaces.description: Kovur is a western suburb of Chennai, India. It is located in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu and is situated at a distance of about 5 kilometers from Porur on the Porur-Kundrathur Road.

topPlaces.name: Kovur Sundareswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Sundareswarar Temple at Kovur, a suburb of Chennai, India is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Shiva. Constructed in about 965 CE, the presiding deity of the temple is Sundareswarar, a form of Shiva and the presiding goddess is Soundarambikai.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kamakshi Amman Temple, Mangadu?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kamakshi Amman Temple, Mangadu is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kamakshi Amman Temple, Mangadu?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (8 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (19 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kamakshi Amman Temple, Mangadu?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kamakshi Amman Temple, Mangadu at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kamakshi Amman Temple, Mangadu?
faq.a: Stays in Kamakshi Amman Temple, Mangadu start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kamakshi Amman Temple, Mangadu?
faq.a: The top attractions are Velleswarar Temple, Mangadu, Mangadu, Sikkarayapuram, Kumananchavadi, Meenakshi Academy of Higher Education and Research.
